2026-04-19T06:02:47.9577476Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9578002Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9578428Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9578807Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9579139Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9579582Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9579753Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-19T06:02:47.9580119Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9580353Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9580566Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9580861Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-19T06:02:47.9581126Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-19T06:04:56.1107316Z Now I have a clear picture. The `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` beta.2 release (triggered by PR #58335) introduced significant new features in PR #58314 that are not documented in the README:
2026-04-19T06:04:56.1108335Z 
2026-04-19T06:04:57.5828811Z 1. `x-agent-session-id` response header on all endpoints (spec §8) — not mentioned
2026-04-19T06:04:57.5830130Z 2. Deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256) — not documented
2026-04-19T06:05:27.5422961Z 3. New observability features (startup config logging, FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy, URL masking, isolation key presence logging) — not in troubleshooting/key concepts
2026-04-19T06:05:27.5426253Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:28.5834940Z ● create_issue (MCP: safeoutputs) · title: "[AgentServer] Docs: Document x-agent-session-id header …
2026-04-19T06:05:28.5837240Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-19T06:05:28.5837697Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:31.5816003Z ● dispatch_triage (MCP: safeoutputs)
2026-04-19T06:05:31.5816571Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-19T06:05:31.5816810Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:34.7450514Z Filed issue **[AgentServer] Docs: Document x-agent-session-id header and observability features added in Responses beta.2** and dispatched triage.
2026-04-19T06:05:34.7451403Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:35.7242368Z **Summary of gaps found in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README:**
2026-04-19T06:05:35.7253090Z - `x-agent-session-id` response header (spec §8) — undocumented; clients need to know the resolution priority order
2026-04-19T06:05:35.7259684Z - Deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256 algorithm) — only in CHANGELOG, not README
2026-04-19T06:05:36.2236291Z - Observability stack from beta.2 (startup logging, per-endpoint structured logging, storage URL masking, isolation key presence flags) — Troubleshooting section still references only OpenTelemetry traces

2026-04-19T06:05:39.6869311Z

2026-04-19T06:05:39.6870209Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6870518Z ✓ web_fetch(url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/..., max_length: 15000)
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6870827Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6871080Z ✓ web_fetch(url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/..., max_length: 15000)
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6871371Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6871868Z ◆ Now let me look at the CHANGELOG for Core, Responses, and Invocations to see what was released and what's currently undocumented in the READMEs:
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6872424Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6872697Z   The recent large PR #58314 introduced several significant features:
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6873019Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6874317Z   1. **Startup configuration logging** - Core, Responses, Invocations all log startup config. This is a new feature but I can see the Core README's troubleshooting section mentions `AddAgentServerLogging()`/`UseAgentServerLogging()`, but startup logging itself is not documented in the "Key concepts" or "Troubleshooting" sections.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6875488Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6875809Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6876816Z   2. **`x-agent-session-id` response header** - All protocol endpoints now return this header, but it's missing from the Responses and Invocations documentation. The session ID itself is derived deterministically via SHA-256, which also isn't mentioned anywhere in the READMEs.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6878531Z   3. **`ServerVersionRegistry`** (renamed from `ServerUserAgentRegistry`) and the new logging extension methods are referenced in the Core README's Key concepts section, but the documentation appears incomplete.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6879729Z   4. **New logging fields** - Log entries now include isolation key presence and storage URL masking, neither of which are documented.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6881005Z   5. **Minimal troubleshooting coverage** - The Responses README's troubleshooting section doesn't address structured logging or the new observability features, and while the Core README has a troubleshooting section, it's sparse on startup configuration logging details.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6881972Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6882294Z   Let me check the changelogs to see what's actually new versus what was already there.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6882780Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6884876Z   Looking at the commit history, PR #58314 is where the real changes happened—both READMEs were updated with the message about fixing documentation deviations from implementation. The key features that might be undocumented include startup configuration logging, the `x-agent-session-id` response header (a spec requirement), deterministic session ID derivation using SHA-256, and those isolation key presence flags in logs that are security-conscious but not yet documented.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6886938Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6887178Z   S... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2609 chars]
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6887412Z

2026-04-19T06:05:39.6888064Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6888347Z ✓ web_fetch(max_length: 8000, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/...)
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6888657Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6889059Z ◆ Now I have a good picture of the documentation state. Let me assess what the Responses README is missing:
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6889525Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6889725Z   From the CHANGELOG for beta.2:
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6890140Z   1. **`x-agent-session-id` response header** on all endpoints - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6890880Z   2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6891357Z   3. **Isolation key presence logging** - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6891801Z   4. **Startup configuration logging** - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6892235Z   5. **FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy** - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6892646Z   6. **Storage URL masking** - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6892950Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6893108Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6893433Z   7. **Server version User-Agent on storage requests** - NOT in Responses README
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6894015Z   8. **Inbound request logging** - Only briefly mentioned in the intro, no actual details provided
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6894463Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6896156Z   The core gap is that the Responses README completely omits the observability features introduced in beta.2—the session ID header behavior, the deterministic derivation algorithm, and the structured logging capabilities that developers need for debugging and production monitoring. I should check if the Core README has similar gaps.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6897381Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6901466Z   Looking at Core, the `ServerVersionRegistry` is mentioned only in passing through AgentHostBuilder, startup configuration logging isn't documented, and the Troubleshooting section mentions the logging methods without explaining what they actually capture. The biggest documentation hole is definitely in Responses—it's missing the `x-agent-session-id` header documentation (what it contains, how it's derived, fallback behavior) and all the new logging features like startup config logging, endpoint logging, storage request logging with URL masking, and isolation key presence logging. These are spec requirements that clients might depend on, so I should file an issue for the Responses package.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6903880Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6904628Z   The version bump commit `ba9a25f0` just triggered the workflow, but the real changes came from PR #58314. Since beta.2 just released and the README doesn't reflect those new features, filing an issue for Responses makes sense.
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6905362Z 
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6906308Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. The `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` beta.2 release (triggered by PR #58335) introduced significant new features in PR #58314 that are not documented in the README:
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6907025Z   
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6907412Z   1. `x-agent-session-id` response header on all endpoints (spec §8) — not mentioned
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6907951Z   2. Deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256) — not documented
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6908821Z   3. New observability features (startup config logging, FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy, URL masking, isolation key presence logging) — not in troubleshooting/key concepts
2026-04-19T06:05:39.6909432Z

2026-04-19T06:05:39.6911859Z

hex\\n- Priority: request payload → env var → deterministic hash → random\\n- Partition hint extracted from conversation_id or previous_response_id\\n- Default agent name 'server-default-agent' when null/empty\\n\\n11 unit tests + 8 protocol tests updated for new format.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* chore: remove Python references ID.\\nUses LoggerMessage source generation for high-performance structured\\nlogging. Request start logs at Information level; successful completion\\nat Information; 4xx/5xx responses at Warning. Query strings are\\ndeliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add handler-level diagnostic logging to Responses endpoints\\n\\nAdd Info-level structured logs to all Responses API endpoints:\\n- POST /responses: responseId, streaming, background, store, model,\\n  conversationId, previousResponseId (moved after ID generation)\\n- POST /responses (non-streaming): final status and output count\\n- POST /responses (background): signal handoff with status\\n- GET /responses: responseId, retrieved status and output count\\n- POST /responses/{id}/cancel: responseId and result status\\n- DELETE /responses/{id}: responseId and success\\n- GET /responses/{id}/input_items: responseId\\n- Orchestrator: handler type name on invocation\\n\\nThese logs complement the Core inbound middleware (HTTP-level) with\\nresponse-specific context for debugging storage deserialization issues,\\nhandler failures, and request tracing across the full pipeline.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update release dates to the pipeline.\\n- ResponseEndpointHandler: cache GetConversationId() result to avoid\\n  repeated JSON parsing across partition key, logging, and session ID.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot endpoint response\\nMUST include x-agent-session-id as a response header.\\n\\nResponses protocol:\\n- Add SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter endpoint filter (outermost, wraps\\n  exception filter) that reads resolved session ID from HttpContext.Items\\n  and falls back to FoundryEnvironment.SessionId\\n- Store resolved session ID in HttpContext.Items from POST (after B39\\n  resolution), GET, Cancel, and SSE replay handler paths\\n- 12 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints, error paths, and\\n  env var fallback\\n\\nInvocations protocol:\\n- Add x-agent-session-id header to GET /invocations/{id} and\\n  POST /invocations/{id}/cancel (POST /invocations already had it)\\n- Add header to GET /invocations/docs/openapi.json with env var fallback\\n- 2 new endpoint tests + header assertions on existing GET/Cancel tests\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Rename ServerUserAgent to ServerVersion and restructure middleware extensions\\n\\nRename all ServerUserAgent* types, files, methods, and properties to\\nServerVersion* — the header identifies the server version stack, not a\\nuser-agent string.\\n\\nType renames:\\n- ServerUserAgentRegistry → ServerVersionRegistry\\n- ServerUserAgentMiddleware → ServerVersionMiddleware\\n- AgentHostBuilder.UserAgentRegistry → VersionRegistry\\n\\nExtension method restructure:\\n- AddAgentServerUserAgent() → AddAgentServerVersion() (version only)\\n- UseAgentServerUserAgent() → UseAgentServerVersion()\\n- NEW: AddAgentServerLogging() — registers InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\n- NEW: UseAgentServerLogging() — wires InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\n\\nThe old AddAgentServerUserAgent() bundled both version and logging\\nregistration which was confusing. Now each concern has its own pair of\\nAdd/Use methods. Tier 1/2 (AgentHostBuilder) lights up both\\nautomatically; Tier 3 users call them independently.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update changelogs for session ID header and ServerVersion rename\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add inbound request logging entries to Responses and Invocations changelogs\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Move inbound logging to Features Added in changelogs\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Internalize framework-only telemetry types\\n\\nMake AgentHostTelemetry, InvocationsActivitySource, ResponsesActivitySource,\\nand ResponsesTracingConstants internal. These types are framework plumbing\\nnot needed by handler authors. Reduces public API surface by 128 lines.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Populate session ID in HttpContext.Items for all endpoint paths\\n\\nSSE replay (in-flight), Delete, and InputItems now store the resolved\\nsession ID so SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter uses the actual response's\\nAgentSessionId instead of the FoundryEnvironment fallback.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add session ID header assertions to Delete and InputItems tests\\n\\nBoth tests now set FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID, assert the header is\\npresent, and verify its value — matching the Cancel test pattern.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Log isolation key presence on endpoint handlers and storage requests\\n\\nAll Responses endpoint handler logs now include HasUserIsolationKey and\\nHasChatIsolationKey booleans (values are never logged).\\n\\nInvocations POST, GET, and Cancel handlers now log the same presence\\nflags alongside the invocation ID.\\n\\nFoundryStorageLoggingPolicy success/failure logs now include whether\\nisolation headers were sent on the outbound storage request.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot
